verses.text: 'The Revelation of Jesus Christ,' shown to John; 'blessed is he that readeth,' for 'the time is at hand.' Unveiling. Christ reveals what must shortly come, and the reader is blessed.
'The Revelation of Jesus Christ,' shown to John; 'blessed is he that readeth,' for 'the time is at hand.' Unveiling. Christ reveals what must shortly come, and the reader is blessed.
'The Revelation of Jesus Christ,' shown to John; 'blessed is he that readeth,' for 'the time is at hand.' Unveiling. Christ reveals what must shortly come, and the reader is blessed.

Grace from Him 'which is, and which was, and which is to come,' and from Jesus, 'the faithful witness,' who 'washed us from our sins in his own blood.' Eternal Lord. The Saviour who cleansed us reigns over all.

Grace from Him 'which is, and which was, and which is to come,' and from Jesus, 'the faithful witness,' who 'washed us from our sins in his own blood.' Eternal Lord. The Saviour who cleansed us reigns over all.
He made us 'kings and priests'; 'Behold, he cometh with clouds.' 'I am Alpha and Omega... the Almighty.' Coming King. The Lord who is first and last will return in glory.
He made us 'kings and priests'; 'Behold, he cometh with clouds.' 'I am Alpha and Omega... the Almighty.' Coming King. The Lord who is first and last will return in glory.
He made us 'kings and priests'; 'Behold, he cometh with clouds.' 'I am Alpha and Omega... the Almighty.' Coming King. The Lord who is first and last will return in glory.
John, in Patmos for the word, heard 'a great voice, as of a trumpet,' bidding him write to the seven churches. The call. Even in exile he received the vision to write.
John, in Patmos for the word, heard 'a great voice, as of a trumpet,' bidding him write to the seven churches. The call. Even in exile he received the vision to write.
John, in Patmos for the word, heard 'a great voice, as of a trumpet,' bidding him write to the seven churches. The call. Even in exile he received the vision to write.
Turning, he saw 'seven golden candlesticks,' and amid them 'one like unto the Son of man,' robed and girt with gold. Christ among the lamps. The Lord walks among His churches.
Turning, he saw 'seven golden candlesticks,' and amid them 'one like unto the Son of man,' robed and girt with gold. Christ among the lamps. The Lord walks among His churches.
His head and hairs 'white like wool,' eyes 'as a flame of fire,' voice 'as the sound of many waters,' His countenance 'as the sun.' Glorious Lord. The risen Christ blazes with majesty.
His head and hairs 'white like wool,' eyes 'as a flame of fire,' voice 'as the sound of many waters,' His countenance 'as the sun.' Glorious Lord. The risen Christ blazes with majesty.
His head and hairs 'white like wool,' eyes 'as a flame of fire,' voice 'as the sound of many waters,' His countenance 'as the sun.' Glorious Lord. The risen Christ blazes with majesty.
John fell as dead; He laid His hand on him: 'Fear not; I am... he that liveth, and was dead,' having 'the keys of hell and of death.' Living Lord. The conqueror of death calms our fear.
John fell as dead; He laid His hand on him: 'Fear not; I am... he that liveth, and was dead,' having 'the keys of hell and of death.' Living Lord. The conqueror of death calms our fear.
Write what you have seen; 'the seven stars are the angels of the seven churches,' the candlesticks the churches. The key. Christ holds His churches and their messengers.
